claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 20 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 7 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 7 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child or children you're claiming for.**

**How old is your child?**

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you have multiple children, we can go through each one. Let's start with one child first - please tell me their age (or age category).

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, I'd like to check for all three of my kids actually. I've got Alex who's 7, Charlie is also 7, and then Blake who's 20.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know about all three of your children! Let me work through the eligibility for each of them.

Let's start with **Alex (age 7)** and **Charlie (age 7)** first, as they're both in the same age category.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Since Alex and Charlie are both 7 years old (under 16), they automatically qualify based on age. At age 7, they wouldn't be in paid employment, so I can proceed with the next check.

**Are Alex and Charlie working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

I expect the answer is yes since they're only 7 years old, but I just need to confirm this to continue the assessment.

---

Regarding **Blake (age 20)**: Unfortunately, children aged 20 or over are generally not eligible for Child Benefit. I'll provide full details on Blake's situation once we complete the assessment for Alex and Charlie.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Right, yeah, neither of the little ones work at all - they're only 7! But Blake does work full-time at the supermarket.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That makes complete sense! Let me continue the assessment for Alex and Charlie.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question for Alex and Charlie:**

Are Alex and Charlie **NOT receiving any benefits in their own right** (such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit)?

At age 7, this would be very unusual, but I need to confirm - are Alex and Charlie free from receiving such benefits themselves?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they don't get any benefits in their own names - they're just kids.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Exactly, that's what I expected! Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question for Alex and Charlie:**

Are Alex and Charlie **NOT in any apprenticeship programmes?**

Again, at age 7, I'd expect they're not in apprenticeships - can you confirm they're not enrolled in any apprenticeship schemes?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Of course not, they're 7 years old! They're just in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! My apologies for the obvious questions - I just need to work through each step. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now we're moving on to questions about **you as the claimant**.

**Do you meet UK residency requirements?**

You can qualify if **ANY** of the following apply to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have **pre-settled status** AND are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold)
4. You have **pre-settled status** AND are actively seeking work
5. You have **pre-settled status** with sufficient resources to support yourself
6. You have **pre-settled status** AND are a student with sufficient resources
7. You have **pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. You're a **Crown servant** living abroad

Do any of these apply to you? For most people, option 1 (normally living in the UK) is the most common.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, living in Manchester covers the residency requirement!

**Next question: Are you responsible for Alex and Charlie?**

You can qualify if **ANY** of the following apply:

1. **You live with the children**
2. **You contribute to their upkeep** (at least the same amount as Child Benefit, through things like food, clothes, pocket money, housing, or presents)
3. **You pay maintenance** for the children under a court order or agreement

Do any of these apply to you for Alex and Charlie?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they both live at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Charlie?**

- **No one else is claiming** for them
- **Someone else was claiming**, but you've agreed that you will now be the one to claim
- **There's a dispute** - someone else is also trying to claim and you haven't agreed who should receive it

Which of these applies to your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Where are Alex and Charlie currently living?**

- **At home with you** (normal living situation)
- **In hospital**
- **In local authority care**
- **In a foster care arrangement**

Which of these describes their current situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex is in a foster care arrangement - I'm fostering Alex. Charlie just lives at home with me normally.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that - it's helpful to know the different situations for each child. Let me check the rules for both.

**For Alex (fostered):**

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**For Alex (the fostered child):**

Is the local council **NOT paying** towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?

In other words, are you fostering Alex **without** receiving payments from the council for their care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right - I don't get anything from the council for fostering Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Alex is **ELIGIBLE** for Child Benefit! 

Now let me check on **Charlie** (who lives at home with you normally):

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**For Charlie (living at home normally):**

Is the local council **NOT paying** towards Charlie's accommodation or maintenance?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything for Charlie.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Charlie is also **ELIGIBLE** for Child Benefit!

Now I have all the information I need to give you the final outcome for all three children.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## 🎉 Child Benefit Eligibility Results

Here's a summary of the eligibility assessment for all three of your children:

| Child | Age | Result |
|-------|-----|--------|
| **Alex** | 7 | ✅ **ELIGIBLE** |
| **Charlie** | 7 | ✅ **ELIGIBLE** |
| **Blake** | 20 | ❌ **NOT ELIGIBLE** |

---

### Details:

**✅ Alex (age 7) - ELIGIBLE**
Even though you're fostering Alex, you can claim Child Benefit because the council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance. All other criteria are met.

**✅ Charlie (age 7) - ELIGIBLE**
Charlie meets all eligibility requirements - they're under 16, living with you at home, and the council isn't paying towards their care.

**❌ Blake (age 20) - NOT ELIGIBLE**
Unfortunately, Child Benefit is only available for children under 20. The benefit can continue up to age 19 only if the child is in approved education or training, but Blake is 20 and working full-time, so they no longer qualify.

---

### Next Steps for Alex and Charlie:

1. **Apply online** or by post using **form CH2**
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and each child
3. **Provide birth certificates** (or adoption certificates) for the children
4. ⚠️ **Be aware**: If you or your partner earns over **£60,000**, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit Charge
5. Payments are usually made **every 4 weeks** (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

📎 For more information and to apply: [https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim)

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex (age 7) is ELIGIBLE. Although Alex is in a foster care arrangement, you confirmed that the local council is NOT paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. Alex is under 16, not in paid work, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in an apprenticeship, you live in the UK, you are responsible for Alex, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

Charlie (age 7) is ELIGIBLE. Charlie is under 16, not in paid work, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in an apprenticeship, lives at home with you normally, you live in the UK, you are responsible for Charlie, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them, and the council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (age 20) is INELIGIBLE. Children aged 20 or over do not qualify for Child Benefit. The upper age limit for Child Benefit is 19 (and only if in approved education or training). Since Blake is 20 and works full-time at a supermarket, they do not meet the age requirements for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for two of their three children. Alex (7, fostered without council payments) and Charlie (7, living at home) both meet all eligibility criteria. Blake (20, working full-time) is not eligible due to being over the maximum age limit for Child Benefit.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

Charlie (age 7) is ELIGIBLE. Charlie is under 16, not in paid work, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in an apprenticeship, lives at home with you normally, you live in the UK, you are responsible for Charlie, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them, and the council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (age 20) is INELIGIBLE. Children aged 20 or over do not qualify for Child Benefit. The upper age limit for Child Benefit is 19 (and only if in approved education or training). Since Blake is 20 and works full-time at a supermarket, they do not meet the age requirements for Child Benefit.
